findings.evidence: Grep/line hits: line 55 defines `blockIndicatorProd`; lines 60-62 provide only `blockIndicatorProd_eq_indicator`; line 75 handles only the `Fin 0` special case. There is no `_apply`/`*_def` theorem for the product form advertised in the docstring.
findings.fix: Add a pointwise product characterization, preferably `@[simp] theorem blockIndicatorProd_apply ... : blockIndicatorProd X k C ω = ∏ i, (C i).indicator (fun _ => (1 : ℝ)) (X (k i) ω)`, and use it in the existing proofs instead of unfolding.
findings.issue: `blockIndicatorProd` is public and intended as a finite product integrand, but the only general exported characterization rewrites it to a cylinder indicator; downstream code cannot rewrite `blockIndicatorProd X k C ω` to `∏ i, (C i).indicator ...` without unfolding the definition body.

summary: The process operations have enough coordinate API, but the new indicator-product definition still lacks the public product-form lemma needed to use it without unfolding its hidden body.
